description: Promise.allSettled — Separate alreadyCalled State Per onFulfilled/onRejected Function Allows Paired Callback Re-Entry After Result Array Exposed → OOB Write (CVE-2025-4918, Pwn2Own 2025). CVE-2025-4918 (Pwn2Own 2025, sec-critical). A subtle bug in the Promise.allSettled implementation allows an out-of-bounds write on the result array. The spec requires a shared 'alreadyCalled' record between onFulfilled/onRejected function pairs, but SpiderMonkey stores the state separately for each function and uses a check against the result array to detect double-invocation. When the last element's first callback resolves all promises, the result array is exposed to JavaScript. Malicious JS can then invoke the paired callback, which accesses the already-exposed array making unsafe assumptions about its length, writing beyond the allocated bounds. The fix makes the second function of each pair reference the first, sharing state properly.

fixes.description: Bug 1966612 - Fix promise combinator function state.
